briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting systemic risks in the BaaS model.

- **[Apple unveils contactless payments via Tap to Pay on iPhone](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2022/02/apple-unveils-contactless-payments-via-tap-to-pay-on-iphone/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple introduced Tap to Pay on iPhone, enabling merchants to accept contactless payments without additional hardware, transforming the payments landscape.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments industry globally.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving increasingly unconventional credit card products with novel rewards, structures, and embedded features.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to an open banking framework, a foundational moment still shaping Canada's ongoing consumer-driven banking rollout.

- **[How Ontario's innovation hubs scale startups across the province](https://betakit.com/how-ontarios-innovation-hubs-scale-startups-across-the-province/)** — *BetaKit* — Ontario's network of regional innovation hubs is playing a key role in helping startups grow and scale across the province.

- **[Boreal Ventures launches second seed fund with $43-million first close](https://betakit.com/boreal-ventures-launches-second-seed-fund-with-43-million-first-close/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian deep-tech-focused VC firm Boreal Ventures has announced a $43-million first close on its second seed fund, signaling continued investor appetite for early-stage Canadian startups.

- **[Canada's annual inflation rate fell to 1.8% in February, war's impact not yet reflected](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/canada-annual-inflation-rate-february-2026-9.7130104?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's inflation eased to 1.8% in February 2026, though economists warn that global conflict-driven pressures have yet to be fully captured in the data.

- **[Canada's benchmark home price fell 4.8% in February compared to last year, says CREA](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/crea-numbers-february-2026-9.7130835?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The Canadian Real Estate Association reports a 4.8% year-over-year decline in benchmark home prices in February, with ongoing affordability pressures shaping consumer financial behaviour.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Boreal Ventures launches second seed fund with $43-million first close](https://betakit.com/boreal-ventures-launches-second-seed-fund-with-43-million-first-close/)** — *BetaKit* — A fresh $43M seed fund in the Canadian VC landscape is a meaningful signal for Atlantic fintech founders seeking early-stage capital, as access to venture funding remains a persistent ecosystem gap in the region.

- **[How Ontario's innovation hubs scale startups across the province](https://betakit.com/how-ontarios-innovation-hubs-scale-startups-across-the-province/)** — *BetaKit* — Ontario's hub-based startup scaling model offers a compelling blueprint for Atlantic Canada's own ecosystem builders working to grow fintech and tech companies across four provinces.

- **[Thinking of moving to a more 'affordable' part of the country? Consider this](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/affordability-canada-provinces-factors-9.7126052?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — As Canadians weigh relocation to more affordable provinces, Atlantic Canada's lower cost of living becomes a draw — creating both opportunity and urgency for regional fintech solutions addressing financial inclusion and access.
